behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "embedding model to convert the query into a vector" + "correct dimensionality for the vector database index." 
Slot B: Boundary signal is model-output contract at the embedding API -> vector-index interface. 
Slot C: Integration with a stubbed embedding response can always return the expected length, so it can miss real model/index dimension mismatch failures. 
MOCK-MISS: This is not just one local function; failure appears at the embedding-output handoff boundary. REAL-INFRA: Usually an external model API call is involved. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, stubs can mask true output shape from the real model/provider config. LLM-DEP: Yes, behavior depends on a real AI/ML output property (vector shape). MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior checks a single model step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. Final label: Agentic, based on Slot B (AI output contract boundary) and Slot C (lower-level mocks hide real mismatch).
behaviors.test_description: Run a test against the real embedding model configured for production and the real index schema metadata; assert returned vector length exactly matches index dimension, and fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "similarity search against a vector database" and "ordered by descending cosine similarity score." 
Slot B: Boundary signal is retrieval-service wiring and score/order handoff between search module and ranking response formatter. 
Slot C: Unit-testing only a sort helper misses failures where DB adapter returns scores in unexpected field/order or service fails to preserve returned ordering. 
MOCK-MISS: Failure is at module interaction (search client -> service -> response), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: Not inherently dependent on OS/network quirks if tested with in-process/local vector store fixture. MOCK-HIDE: Full mocking can hide adapter mapping bugs, but an integration fixture catches them. LLM-DEP: No, this behavior is pre-LLM ranking logic. MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text focuses on score ordering semantics, not real network/subprocess/OS effects; Agentic rejected because no model-output quality judgment is being tested. Final label: Integration, based on Slot B (module handoff) and Slot C (unit miss-proof).
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with known vectors, execute similarity search through service + repository modules, and assert returned list is strictly non-increasing by cosine score.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "A re-ranking step uses an LLM to reorder" and "when the query has nuanced intent." 
Slot B: Boundary signal is correctness of a real LLM decision about contextual relevance. 
Slot C: Any deterministic stub can be forced to reorder, so it cannot prove the real model actually performs nuanced-intent re-ranking. 
MOCK-MISS: The failure is not simple orchestration; it is model judgment quality at re-ranking boundary. REAL-INFRA: Real model/API call path is needed to expose true behavior. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocked outputs trivially satisfy reorder assertions. LLM-DEP: Explicitly yes. MULTI-STEP: No, single agentic decision point (reranker) is under test. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior names one LLM re-ranking step, not a multi-agentic chain. Final label: Agentic, based on Slot B (real model judgment) and Slot C (stub hides failure).
behaviors.test_description: Use the real LLM reranker on a curated nuanced-intent query set where baseline similarity order is known; assert at least one pairwise order change occurs in expected cases versus raw similarity ranking.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "no close matches in the database returns an empty result set" 
Slot B: Boundary signal is threshold/filter logic across retrieval module and API response shaping. 
Slot C: Unit tests on threshold function alone miss failures where repository/search layer still emits items and service fails to enforce empty-output contract. 
MOCK-MISS: Yes, behavior depends on module handoff and state, so Unit can miss it. REAL-INFRA: No explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess; local seeded store with low-similarity fixtures can reproduce failure. MOCK-HIDE: Pure mocks may hide it, but integration fixture is sufficient. LLM-DEP: Not required by behavior text; it specifies no-close-match filtering outcome, not model relevance judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because the behavior text does not require validating LLM decision quality; it requires enforcing empty-set contract when similarity is below match threshold. Final label: Integration, based on Slot B (cross-module filtering boundary) and Slot C (unit miss-proof).
behaviors.test_description: Populate a local test index with vectors intentionally far from query embeddings, run the retrieval service path (including thresholding), and assert API payload contains zero results.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case." 
Slot B: Boundary signal is full runtime path timing across HTTP request handling, embedding call, vector search, and rerank call. 
Slot C: Integration tests with mocked dependencies can meet 2s while production fails due to real network/API latency and process overhead. 
MOCK-MISS: Yes, true latency failures emerge only with real infra interaction. REAL-INFRA: Yes, real HTTP/network/service timing is the core failure mode. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocks collapse latency variance and hide regressions. LLM-DEP: Output quality is not under test; only timing is. MULTI-STEP: Multiple calls exist, but not multiple agentic decision-quality assertions. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior asserts a performance SLO, not correctness of model-generated content; Workflow rejected because no chained agentic judgment criterion is being validated. Final label: System, based on Slot B (end-to-end runtime boundary) and Slot C (mocked timing miss-proof).
behaviors.test_description: Run median-latency load test against deployed API with real dependency calls (or production-equivalent services) over representative queries; measure p50 end-to-end latency and assert p50 < 2000 ms.
